triage_logic.clinical_status: Mild depressive symptoms with minimal functional impairment
triage_logic.action_recommendation: Psychoeducation + Psychotherapy (CBT/IPT preferred) OR Antidepressant medication based on patient preference
triage_logic.notes: Psychotherapy may be preferred initial treatment for mild depression with psychosocial stressors

triage_logic.clinical_status: Moderate depressive symptoms with noticeable functional impairment, may include sleep/appetite disturbance
triage_logic.action_recommendation: Antidepressant medication (SSRI first-line) + Psychotherapy (CBT/IPT). Monitor for 4-6 weeks for response
triage_logic.referral_tier: Psychiatrist Mandatory + Clinical Psychologist for combined treatment
triage_logic.treatment_duration: Acute phase: 6-9 months minimum from symptom remission. Continuation phase: 16-24 weeks after remission
triage_logic.notes: If <25% improvement after 4 weeks, consider dose optimization or switch. If <50% improvement after 6-8 weeks with max tolerable dose, switch antidepressant

triage_logic.clinical_status: Severe depression with marked functional impairment, may include psychotic features, catatonia, severe weight loss, marked psychomotor changes
triage_logic.action_recommendation: Antidepressant + Antipsychotic (if psychotic features) OR ECT. Hospitalization often required. Combination pharmacotherapy + ECT for acute stabilization
triage_logic.notes: ECT indicated for: severe depression with suicidality, psychotic features, catatonia, poor oral intake, past good response to ECT. Treatment-resistant cases may require augmentation with lithium or thyroid supplements. Adhere to MHCA 2017 regarding ECT consent.

triggers: Active suicidal ideation with plan or intent
Severe medical comorbidity making outpatient unsafe
triage_logic.action_recommendation: Immediate Hospitalization. ECT + Pharmacotherapy. 24/7 monitoring. Ensure family supervision. Remove means of self-harm
triage_logic.notes: Adhere to MHCA 2017 regarding ECT consent and involuntary admission procedures

special_populations.elderly: Avoid TCAs (high adverse effects). Prefer SSRIs. Nortriptyline for severe cases. ECT effective with rapid response
special_populations.pregnancy_postpartum: Avoid first trimester if possible. ECT safe during pregnancy. Address breastfeeding concerns
special_populations.children_adolescents: Fluoxetine first-line SSRI. CBT and IPT effective
special_populations.comorbid_substance_use: Detoxify before antidepressant. Avoid benzodiazepines. Monitor hepatic function

condition_name: Generalized Anxiety Disorder and Panic Disorder

panic_override_logic.description: If panic attack frequency is high (≥2 attacks per week), upgrade severity to Moderate/Severe regardless of GAD-7 score
panic_override_logic.action: Upgrade to Moderate severity minimum. Consider SSRI/SNRI + Benzodiazepine for acute attacks + CBT

triage_logic.clinical_status: Mild anxiety symptoms with minimal functional impairment
triage_logic.action_recommendation: Psychoeducation + CBT + Relaxation techniques. Consider pharmacotherapy if no response
triage_logic.notes: Psychotherapy preferred as first-line for mild GAD

triage_logic.clinical_status: Moderate anxiety with noticeable functional impairment, persistent worry, OR frequent panic attacks
triage_logic.action_recommendation: SSRI/SNRI + CBT combination. Benzodiazepines short-term (2-4 weeks max) for acute symptoms. For panic: mouth-dissolving short-acting benzodiazepines for acute attacks
triage_logic.treatment_duration: 12-24 months minimum after symptom control. Benzodiazepines: maximum 2-4 weeks
triage_logic.notes: Start SSRIs at low dose and titrate slowly to avoid initial anxiety worsening. Benzodiazepines only for short-term use due to dependence risk. For panic disorder: treat for at least 6-8 months or longer to prevent relapses

triage_logic.clinical_status: Severe anxiety with marked functional impairment, may include frequent panic attacks, agoraphobia, severe avoidance
triage_logic.action_recommendation: SSRI/SNRI at therapeutic doses + Intensive CBT + Short-term benzodiazepine. Consider hospitalization if severe functional impairment
triage_logic.notes: Treatment-resistant cases may require augmentation with Buspirone, Pregabalin, or low-dose atypical antipsychotics. Combination of CBT and anxiolytic medication has best treatment outcomes

triage_logic.action_recommendation: Immediate psychiatric evaluation. Consider hospitalization. Aggressive pharmacotherapy + crisis intervention
triage_logic.notes: Adhere to MHCA 2017 regarding involuntary admission if patient poses risk to self

notes: Indian context: High comorbidity with depression (up to 60%). Screen for depression in all GAD patients. Benzodiazepine dependence is a concern - use judiciously and for shortest duration possible. Panic disorder requires longer treatment (6-8 months minimum)

severity_scale.name: PANSS (Positive and Negative Syndrome Scale)
severity_scale.scoring_logic: Positive symptoms (hallucinations, delusions), Negative symptoms (flat affect, avolition), General psychopathology. Severity based on functional impairment and symptom intensity

triage_logic.clinical_status: First episode of psychotic symptoms, duration <2 years
triage_logic.action_recommendation: Immediate antipsychotic initiation. Psychoeducation to family. Early intervention critical for better prognosis

mandatory_labs.monitoring: Metabolic monitoring: Weight monthly for first 3 months, then quarterly. Fasting glucose and lipid profile at 3 months, then annually. Monitor for extrapyramidal symptoms at each visit

triage_logic.notes: Use lowest effective dose. Monitor for side effects closely. Family psychoeducation essential

triage_logic.clinical_status: Acute worsening of psychotic symptoms, agitation, behavioral disturbance
triage_logic.action_recommendation: Hospitalization often required. Antipsychotic optimization. Short-term benzodiazepines for agitation. ECT if catatonia or treatment-resistant

mandatory_labs.monitoring: Metabolic monitoring: Weight weekly during hospitalization, then monthly. Fasting glucose and lipid profile at 3 months, 6 months, then annually. Monitor for extrapyramidal symptoms, tardive dyskinesia at each visit

triage_logic.notes: Address medication non-compliance. Consider long-acting injectables. Rule out substance-induced psychosis. Adhere to MHCA 2017 regarding ECT consent and involuntary admission

triage_logic.clinical_status: Symptoms controlled, in remission or partial remission, on maintenance treatment
triage_logic.action_recommendation: Continue same antipsychotic at lowest effective dose. Psychosocial rehabilitation. Family support. Vocational training

mandatory_labs.monitoring: Metabolic monitoring: Weight quarterly. Fasting glucose and lipid profile every 6-12 months. Annual ECG. Monitor for extrapyramidal symptoms and tardive dyskinesia at each visit (use AIMS scale)

triage_logic.treatment_duration: Minimum 2-5 years after first episode. Lifelong for multiple episodes
triage_logic.notes: Monitor for relapse signs. Ensure medication compliance. Address negative symptoms and functional impairment

triage_logic.action_recommendation: Immediate Hospitalization. Parenteral antipsychotics. ECT for catatonia. Medical stabilization. 24/7 monitoring
triage_logic.notes: Adhere to MHCA 2017 regarding ECT consent and involuntary admission procedures. Neuroleptic malignant syndrome requires immediate medical intervention

special_populations.treatment_resistant: Clozapine 300-900mg is gold standard. Requires regular blood monitoring: Absolute neutrophil count (ANC) weekly for 18 weeks, then biweekly for 1 year, then monthly. Baseline: ECG, echocardiogram, fasting glucose, lipids. ECT augmentation may help
special_populations.pregnancy: Avoid first trimester if possible. Haloperidol relatively safer. Monitor for gestational diabetes with SGAs
special_populations.elderly: Lower doses (50% of adult dose). Monitor for extrapyramidal symptoms and metabolic effects closely
special_populations.comorbid_substance_use: Address substance use. May worsen prognosis. Integrated treatment approach

notes: Indian context: Family involvement crucial. Stigma is major barrier. Long-acting injectables improve compliance in resource-poor settings. Clozapine underutilized despite being most effective for treatment-resistant cases. Metabolic monitoring often neglected - emphasize importance

severity_scale.name: YMRS (Young Mania Rating Scale) for mania, MADRS for depression
severity_scale.scoring_logic: YMRS: <12=Remission, 12-19=Mild, 20-25=Moderate, >25=Severe mania. Combined with depression severity assessment

triage_logic.clinical_status: Depressive episode in bipolar disorder without psychotic features
triage_logic.action_recommendation: Mood stabilizer + Antidepressant (with caution - risk of switch to mania). Psychotherapy. Monitor for manic switch

mandatory_labs.lithium_monitoring: Serum lithium levels: After 5 days of stable dose (12-14 hours post-dose), then every 3-4 months. Renal function: Every 2-3 months for first 6 months, then every 6-12 months. Thyroid function: Once or twice in first 6 months, then every 6-12 months. Monitor for polyuria, polydipsia, tremor, dysarthria, ataxia at each visit
mandatory_labs.valproate_monitoring: Haemogram and LFT: Not routine, may do every 6-12 months. Serum valproate levels: After 5 days of stable dose (12 hours post-dose for immediate release, 21-24 hours for extended release)

triage_logic.notes: Never use antidepressant monotherapy - risk of manic switch. Lamotrigine particularly effective for bipolar depression. Slow titration of lamotrigine essential to prevent Stevens-Johnson syndrome

triage_logic.clinical_status: Elevated mood, increased energy, mild functional impairment, no psychotic features
triage_logic.action_recommendation: Mood stabilizer initiation or optimization. Discontinue antidepressants if on them. Psychoeducation. Outpatient management possible

mandatory_labs.lithium_monitoring: Serum lithium levels every 3-4 months once stabilized. Renal and thyroid function every 6-12 months

triage_logic.notes: Monitor for progression to full mania. Ensure medication compliance

triage_logic.clinical_status: Severe manic symptoms with marked functional impairment, may include psychotic features, severe agitation
triage_logic.action_recommendation: Hospitalization required. Mood stabilizer + Antipsychotic. Benzodiazepines for agitation. ECT if treatment-resistant or severe

mandatory_labs.lithium_monitoring: Serum lithium levels: After 5 days of stable dose, then every 3-4 months. Check earlier if toxicity suspected (tremor, dysarthria, ataxia, confusion, vomiting). Renal function every 2-3 months initially, then every 6-12 months. Thyroid function every 6-12 months
mandatory_labs.valproate_monitoring: Haemogram and LFT every 6-12 months. Monitor for signs of hepatic dysfunction, haematological dysfunction
mandatory_labs.antipsychotic_monitoring: Weight weekly during hospitalization, then monthly. Fasting glucose and lipid profile at 3 months, 6 months, then annually. Monitor for extrapyramidal symptoms

triage_logic.notes: Combination therapy (mood stabilizer + antipsychotic) more effective than monotherapy for acute mania. Monitor lithium levels regularly. Lithium toxicity signs: tremor, dysarthria, ataxia, confusion, vomiting, diarrhea. Adhere to MHCA 2017 regarding ECT consent and involuntary admission

triage_logic.clinical_status: Simultaneous manic and depressive symptoms, high suicide risk
triage_logic.action_recommendation: Hospitalization often needed. Mood stabilizer + Atypical antipsychotic. Avoid antidepressants. Close suicide monitoring

mandatory_labs.valproate_monitoring: Haemogram and LFT every 6-12 months. Serum valproate levels after 5 days of stable dose
mandatory_labs.antipsychotic_monitoring: Weight monthly. Fasting glucose and lipid profile at 3 months, then every 6-12 months

triage_logic.notes: Highest suicide risk among bipolar states. Aggressive treatment needed. Avoid antidepressants - worsen mixed states

triggers: Lithium toxicity (tremor, dysarthria, ataxia, confusion, vomiting)
triage_logic.action_recommendation: Immediate Hospitalization. Parenteral medications. ECT for severe/refractory cases. Medical monitoring for lithium toxicity (stop lithium, IV fluids, check levels)
triage_logic.notes: Adhere to MHCA 2017 regarding ECT consent and involuntary admission procedures. Lithium toxicity requires immediate medical intervention - stop lithium, IV hydration, monitor levels. Neuroleptic malignant syndrome: stop antipsychotic, supportive care, consider dantrolene/bromocriptine

maintenance_treatment.first_line: Lithium (gold standard), Valproate, Lamotrigine (especially for bipolar II and depression prevention)
maintenance_treatment.monitoring: Lithium: levels every 3-6 months, thyroid and renal function annually. Valproate: liver function, CBC every 6-12 months. All: weight, metabolic parameters (glucose, lipids) every 6-12 months
maintenance_treatment.notes: Medication adherence crucial. Psychoeducation about illness and early warning signs. Regular sleep-wake cycle important. Avoid antidepressant monotherapy

notes: Indian context: Lithium remains gold standard and cost-effective. Regular monitoring challenging in rural areas. Family involvement essential for medication compliance and early relapse detection. Educate about salt-restricted diet and dehydration risks with lithium. Valproate contraindicated in women of childbearing age due to teratogenicity

severity_scale.name: Insomnia Severity Index (ISI)
severity_scale.scoring_logic: 0-7=No clinically significant insomnia, 8-14=Subthreshold insomnia, 15-21=Moderate insomnia, 22-28=Severe insomnia

triage_logic.clinical_status: Occasional sleep difficulty with minimal daytime impairment
triage_logic.action_recommendation: Sleep hygiene education + CBT for Insomnia (CBT-I). Avoid pharmacotherapy initially
triage_logic.referral_tier: Self-help resources OR Clinical Psychologist for CBT-I
triage_logic.notes: Sleep hygiene: regular sleep schedule, avoid caffeine/alcohol, comfortable sleep environment, limit screen time before bed, regular exercise (not close to bedtime)

triage_logic.clinical_status: Frequent sleep difficulty with noticeable daytime impairment, present ≥3 nights/week for ≥3 months
triage_logic.action_recommendation: CBT-I (first-line) + Short-term pharmacotherapy if needed. Address underlying causes (anxiety, depression, medical conditions)
triage_logic.referral_tier: Psychiatrist OR Sleep Medicine Specialist + Psychologist for CBT-I
triage_logic.notes: CBT-I more effective long-term than medications. Avoid long-term benzodiazepines due to dependence risk. Rule out sleep apnea (snoring, witnessed apneas, daytime sleepiness), restless legs syndrome. Melatonin especially useful for circadian rhythm disorders and elderly

triage_logic.clinical_status: Severe sleep disturbance with marked daytime dysfunction, may be comorbid with psychiatric/medical conditions
triage_logic.action_recommendation: Comprehensive evaluation for underlying causes. CBT-I + Pharmacotherapy. Address comorbid conditions aggressively
triage_logic.referral_tier: Psychiatrist Mandatory + Sleep Medicine Specialist + Psychologist
triage_logic.notes: Rule out obstructive sleep apnea (requires polysomnography). Address medical comorbidities (chronic pain, thyroid disorders, GERD). Severe insomnia often secondary to other psychiatric conditions - treat primary disorder

triggers: Complete functional breakdown due to sleep deprivation
Dangerous behaviors due to severe daytime impairment
Suspected severe sleep apnea with cardiopulmonary complications
triage_logic.action_recommendation: Immediate psychiatric/medical evaluation. Address underlying emergency condition. Hospitalization if needed for safety
triage_logic.notes: Adhere to MHCA 2017 regarding involuntary admission if patient poses risk to self due to sleep deprivation

special_populations.elderly: Avoid benzodiazepines (fall risk, cognitive impairment, paradoxical agitation). Prefer melatonin 3-5mg, low-dose sedating antidepressants (mirtazapine 7.5-15mg, trazodone 25-50mg). Rule out sleep apnea, restless legs syndrome, medical causes
special_populations.pregnancy: Non-pharmacological approaches preferred. Avoid benzodiazepines in first trimester. Melatonin may be safer option. CBT-I is first-line
special_populations.comorbid_psychiatric: Treat underlying condition (depression, anxiety). Insomnia often improves with treatment of primary disorder. SSRIs may initially worsen insomnia - consider adding short-term hypnotic

notes: Indian context: CBT-I resources limited - emphasize sleep hygiene education. Benzodiazepine overuse is concern - educate about dependence risk. Many patients present late with chronic insomnia. Rule out medical causes: thyroid disorders, chronic pain, GERD, sleep apnea. Yoga, meditation, and pranayama may be helpful adjuncts
